hazards.source: NOAA Sea Level Rise Viewer and Miami-Dade County Flood Zone Maps
hazards.description: Recurrent sunny-day tidal flooding in low-lying areas, particularly Miami Beach, Downtown, and Shorecrest, driven by sea-level rise.
vulnerableGroups: Small business owners in flood-prone commercial districts
hazards.proportionExposedRange: Approximately 30-40% of the city's land area is highly susceptible.
hazards.impact: Significant damage to public infrastructure, devaluation of real estate, and increased flood insurance costs.

hazards.source: National Hurricane Center (NHC) Historical Data and Saffir-Simpson Scale
hazards.description: High frequency of exposure to tropical cyclones, including major hurricanes, with destructive winds, storm surge, and extreme rainfall.
vulnerableGroups: Populations residing in coastal areas exposed to storm surge
Residents of older buildings not built to modern hurricane codes
Individuals dependent on electricity for medical equipment
hazards.proportionExposedRange: 100% of the population and assets are at risk during hurricane season.
hazards.impact: Widespread power outages, severe structural damage, and large-scale business interruption.

hazards.source: National Weather Service (NWS) Heat Index and CDC Climate and Health Data
hazards.description: A rising number of days per year with a heat index exceeding 105F during summer months.
vulnerableGroups: Low-income households without access to or unable to afford air conditioning
hazards.proportionExposedRange: Over 90% of the population is regularly exposed to dangerous heat levels during the summer.
hazards.impact: Increased public health emergencies, reduced labor productivity, and heightened risk of rolling blackouts.

goals.description: Ensure all critical public infrastructure (hospitals, shelters, utilities) is resilient to a Category 4 hurricane storm surge and wind loads by 2040.
Reduce the impact of urban heat islands by increasing tree canopy coverage by 30% and implementing cool pavement standards for 50% of public surfaces by 2035.

actions.title: Miami Forever Bond - Seawall and Stormwater Pump Station Upgrades
coBenefits: Creation of local engineering and construction jobs
Improved water quality in Biscayne Bay

actions.title: Resilient Neighborhoods Program: Cooling Centers and Green Infrastructure

status.otherStatusDetails: Currently active in Little Haiti and Allapattah, with plans for city-wide expansion.

coBenefits: Enhanced public health outcomes for vulnerable groups
Improved neighborhood aesthetics and social cohesion

actions.title: Public Climate Risk Information Campaign

status.otherStatusDetails: Phase 2 of the digital and community outreach program is underway.

coBenefits: Greater public support for adaptation investments

projects.title: Virginia Key Wastewater Treatment Plant Flood Protection and Hardening
Biscayne Bay Mangrove Restoration and Living Shoreline Project
